evals.description: Trigger test: activates on SLO definition question
evals.prompt: Our team just launched a new checkout service and our CTO wants us to define SLOs before we go to GA. We have no historical data yet. Where do we start?

evals.description: Trigger test: activates on toil reduction question
evals.prompt: Our on-call team spends about 10 hours a week manually restarting services, clearing dead-letter queues, and rotating credentials. Is this normal and what should we do about it?

evals.description: Core task: defining availability and latency SLOs with formulas
evals.prompt: I need to write SLOs for our user authentication service. It handles login, token refresh, and password reset. What SLIs should I measure and what are realistic targets for a service at this criticality?

evals.description: Core task: calculating burn rate and setting burn rate alerts
evals.prompt: We have a 99.9% availability SLO on a 30-day window. Last hour our error rate jumped to 2%. How bad is this? When should I page someone vs just create a ticket?

evals.description: Core task: running a blameless postmortem
evals.prompt: We had a 2-hour outage yesterday where our database connection pool was exhausted. The engineer who wrote the connection code is feeling terrible. How do we run a postmortem that actually improves the system without making them feel worse?

evals.description: Core task: designing on-call rotation with escalation policy
evals.prompt: We're a team of 6 engineers about to take on on-call responsibility for the first time. How do we set up a rotation, define escalation policies, and make sure nobody burns out?

evals.description: Core task: capacity planning with headroom
evals.prompt: Our API is currently handling 500 RPS at 60% CPU. We expect 3x traffic growth over the next 6 months based on our sales pipeline. How do we plan capacity and when do we need to act?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns against setting aggressive SLOs without historical data
evals.prompt: Our CEO saw that AWS advertises 99.99% SLAs for their services and wants us to match that for our new product. We have about 50 RPS and 2 engineers. Should we just set a 99.99% SLO?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns against alerting on resource metrics instead of SLO burn
evals.prompt: We have alerts set up for CPU > 80% and memory > 85% and we're getting paged 3-4 times a night even though customers say nothing is wrong. What are we doing wrong?

assertions.value: CPU threshold is the right signal

evals.description: Gotcha: warns against infinite error budget freeze
evals.prompt: Our error budget is at 5% remaining with 2 weeks left in the month. My manager wants to freeze all deploys until next month when the budget resets. But we have a critical security patch that needs to ship. What should we do?

evals.description: Anti-hallucination: does not recommend blaming individuals in postmortems
evals.prompt: We had an outage because a junior engineer pushed directly to main without a review and it took down prod for 30 minutes. The postmortem is tomorrow. Should we call them out so others learn from it?

evals.description: References load: directs to postmortem template for postmortem facilitation
evals.prompt: I've been asked to facilitate our first postmortem next week for a SEV1 that caused 90 minutes of downtime. I've never run one before. Can you walk me through the structure and what questions to ask?
